rationale: Adds `build/patch-casaos-ui.cjs`, a 55-line Node script that rewrites the CasaOS-UI `package.json` dependency `@icewhale/icewhale-files-openapi` to use pnpm's `npm:@yundera/icewhale-files-openapi@1.3.0-alpha133` alias syntax, then performs three precise string replacements in `pnpm-lock.yaml` (importer specifier, packages entry with new sha512 integrity hash, snapshots entry) to keep `--frozen-lockfile` working. The Dockerfile is updated to `COPY ./build/patch-casaos-ui.cjs /tmp/patch-casaos-ui.cjs` and `RUN node /tmp/patch-casaos-ui.cjs` before `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`. This is a legitimate workaround for an upstream npm package removal; closed-without-merge does not imply broken (maintainers likely chose a different mitigation). No cheat pattern.
